description:
## Background

Beadsmith currently renders a snapshot of the Current Workspace's Issue List. Beadwork can change after that snapshot through a local terminal, coding agent, or local `bw sync`; the app then silently shows stale Issues.

Beadwork's durable issue-data version is the commit SHA at the local `refs/heads/beadwork` ref. ADR-0007 chooses a 2-second probe of that ref over a filesystem watcher. This first vertical slice establishes the narrow, complete path from a local ref move to an updated Issue List while one Current Workspace remains selected.

The existing workspace transition protocol already owns Current Workspace selection and its generation. Refreshes inside that selection need a separate monotonic refresh revision: selection generation does not change every time the Beadwork ref moves.

## What to build

While a Current Workspace is selected, an external local change to its Beadwork ref must update All Issues, Ready, and Blocked in the running Beadsmith UI within roughly three seconds, without a manual refresh action or a disruptive loading state.

A user should be able to run `bw create`, `bw close`, or another local Beadwork mutation outside Beadsmith and see the resulting Issue List change in place. Rapid successive mutations must converge to the newest ref tip rather than leave the UI at an older snapshot.

## Technical Approach

- Add a refresh coordinator bound to the already-selected Current Workspace. It probes the fully qualified local ref through `git -C <workspace> rev-parse --verify refs/heads/beadwork^{commit}` every two seconds.
- Reuse the existing subprocess-runner test seam for the git probe and the existing Beadwork loaders for the full Issue List snapshot. Do not read Beadwork's git tree directly.
- Route ref-change requests through one non-overlapping loader. If a ref move is observed while a load is active, mark the request dirty and perform one follow-up load after the active one completes when the current ref still differs.
- Define the refresh event contract at the backend/frontend boundary. A successful event carries the full Issue Explorer data, the workspace path, the observed ref SHA, the existing workspace-selection generation, and a monotonically increasing refresh revision.
- Extend the renderer's existing admission behavior so it accepts only events for the current Workspace and only a revision newer than the currently rendered refresh revision. Do not create a parallel owner for Workspace selection.
- Preserve the existing initial-load behavior. This slice concerns automatic updates after a Current Workspace has a successful snapshot.

## Considerations

- Git refs can be loose or packed; the probe must ask `git` to resolve the ref instead of watching or parsing ref files directly.
- Several ref moves may occur within one two-second interval. The UI only needs to converge to the final local ref tip; it does not need to render every intermediate commit.
- A completion from an older refresh revision must never regress the Issue List after a newer snapshot has rendered.
- This task depends on the workspace-management epic because Current Workspace selection and its generation are already the ownership boundary for refreshes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With one Current Workspace selected, an external local `bw` mutation is reflected in All Issues, Ready, and Blocked within roughly three seconds.
- [ ] The automatic update keeps the existing Issue List visible while the refresh runs; it does not show the initial-load spinner, a toast, or a manual-refresh control.
- [ ] Two or more ref moves during an active load converge to the newest observed ref tip through the dirty follow-up load.
- [ ] Refresh events include workspace identity, workspace-selection generation, observed SHA, and refresh revision.
- [ ] The renderer rejects an event for another Workspace or an older refresh revision.
- [ ] The existing initial Issue List load continues to work unchanged.

## Test Plan

- Unit-test ref probing with the existing fake command runner: unchanged SHA, changed SHA, missing ref, and command failure.
- Unit-test the coordinator with controlled command outputs: no reload when SHA is unchanged, one reload on change, and dirty follow-up when the ref changes during a load.
- Unit-test renderer admission: a newer refresh revision wins and a late older event cannot regress the state.
- Add a slice-level WebDriver proof: launch the real desktop app, mutate Beadwork outside the app, and wait for the new Issue to appear without fixed sleeps.
title: Refresh Current Workspace when the Beadwork ref changes
